category_title: Students who talk out to access peer attention
minimal_context: Craig is in the general classroom during independent work time, expected to complete his assigned tasks.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for independent work. Please start working quietly on your assigned tasks.

STUDENT: (Turns to a peer) Hey, did you see the game last night?

TUTOR: Craig, remember the rules for independent work time.

STUDENT: I am just asking a question. (Turns back to peer) Seriously though, what did you think of that last play?

TUTOR: Please focus on your own assignment.

STUDENT: (Laughs as peer responds back) I know, right? It was crazy! Look at this drawing I did of it.

options.A: Provide assigned tasks using large fonts with supporting pictures, reductions in the number of questions to answer, access to word banks, and speech-generating devices
options.B: Implement independent work activities with less demanding rules for the student to follow during the general classroom independent work time
options.C: Provide opportunities for the student to engage in drawing, painting, water coloring, and pottery making during the independent work time
options.D: Provide specific times for purposeful attention through noncontingent reinforcement (NCR) by scheduling frequent, free access to peer attention throughout the day
